contract.claim_boundary: Tier 7.4d is a pre-registration contract. It authorizes a later held-out scoring preflight/gate, but it does not score v2.4, freeze a new baseline, claim public usefulness, or authorize hardware/native policy transfer.
fail_criteria: A simple fixed threshold, rolling z-score, lag/ridge, or reservoir/ESN policy wins the held-out public family.
v2.4 wins only on private/synthetic diagnostics and not on any public/real-ish held-out family.
Any sham or ablation matches the intact v2.4 result on the claimed family.
The policy collapses into abstain/wait or always-act behavior while gaming utility.
The score requires threshold or cost tuning on the held-out/test subset.
Public data, split, or label-separation artifacts are missing or unverifiable.
contract.hypothesis: If v2.4 learned a general cost-aware policy/action mechanism rather than a private-task trick, it should improve expected utility or regret on at least one held-out public/real-ish action-cost family while separating confidence, memory, recurrent-state, and shuffled-cost shams.
contract.next_gate_if_accepted: Tier 7.4e - Cost-Aware Policy/Action Held-Out Scoring Preflight
nonclaims: not broad anomaly or predictive-maintenance superiority
contract.null_hypothesis: v2.4 does not beat fixed train-only thresholds, rolling policies, online learners, reservoir/ESN policy baselines, or trivial act/abstain controls on held-out action-cost tasks under locked costs and splits.
pass_criteria: v2.4 beats or complements the strongest reproduced baseline on the primary utility metric for at least one public/real-ish held-out family.
The result is not explained by fixed train-only thresholds, rolling z-score, reservoir/ESN policy, always-act, or always-abstain controls.
Shuffled cost, random confidence, confidence-disabled, memory-disabled, and recurrent-state-disabled controls lose on the claimed family.
No-action and always-action collapse are explicitly ruled out by action-rate and recall/precision guards.
Bootstrap confidence intervals or paired effect sizes support the claimed utility delta on the held-out family.
No leakage, same-subset policy selection, or test-label threshold tuning is detected.
contract.question: Does the frozen v2.4 cost-aware policy/action layer preserve a measurable utility advantage on external or held-out action-cost tasks without threshold tuning, leakage, or same-subset policy selection?
split_and_leakage_rules: All thresholds, policy temperatures, abstain costs, and calibration transforms are selected on train/calibration splits only.
No test-label threshold tuning is allowed for any model, baseline, policy, cost, or abstention rule.
No same-subset policy selection: if a policy is selected on a diagnostic subset, confirmation must use disjoint held-out streams/units/windows.
Each action is emitted before the feedback, anomaly label, RUL label, or outcome update for that row/window is visible.
Labels and event windows stay in separate artifacts from online input rows until offline scoring.
Public-source checksums, selected streams/units, split ids, and ignored raw-data-cache paths must be recorded.
Any repair after a failed held-out score returns to a new contract or diagnostic; it cannot overwrite the held-out result.

task_families.heldout_rule: use streams/categories not used to choose same-subset NAB policies
task_families.name: nab_heldout_alarm_action_cost
task_families.source: Numenta Anomaly Benchmark pinned by Tier 7.1g

task_families.heldout_rule: train/calibrate on train units only; test actions emitted before RUL feedback
task_families.name: cmapss_maintenance_action_cost
task_families.source: NASA C-MAPSS FD001 source audited by Tier 7.1b

task_families.claim_role: standardized regression-to-action diagnostic, not public usefulness alone
task_families.heldout_rule: use locked train/calibration/test windows; no retuning on test windows
task_families.name: standard_dynamical_action_cost
task_families.source: locked Mackey-Glass, Lorenz, and NARMA10 streams from the Tier 7.0 scoreboard

task_families.claim_role: mechanism localization only; cannot support public usefulness by itself
task_families.heldout_rule: locked seeds and task parameters before scoring
task_families.name: heldout_synthetic_policy_stress

criteria.name: question/hypothesis/null defined
value: Does the frozen v2.4 cost-aware policy/action layer preserve a measurable utility advantage on external or held-out action-cost tasks without threshold tuning, leakage, or same-subset policy selection?

If v2.4 learned a general cost-aware policy/action mechanism rather than a private-task trick, it should improve expected utility or regret on at least one held-out public/real-ish action-cost family while separating confidence, memory, recurrent-state, and shuffled-cost shams.

v2.4 does not beat fixed train-only thresholds, rolling policies, online learners, reservoir/ESN policy baselines, or trivial act/abstain controls on held-out action-cost tasks under locked costs and splits.

actions include abstain wait and act
cost model locks FP miss latency and maintenance
split rules block test tuning/leakage
value: All thresholds, policy temperatures, abstain costs, and calibration transforms are selected on train/calibration splits only.
No test-label threshold tuning is allowed for any model, baseline, policy, cost, or abstention rule.
No same-subset policy selection: if a policy is selected on a diagnostic subset, confirmation must use disjoint held-out streams/units/windows.
Each action is emitted before the feedback, anomaly label, RUL label, or outcome update for that row/window is visible.
Labels and event windows stay in separate artifacts from online input rows until offline scoring.
Public-source checksums, selected streams/units, split ids, and ignored raw-data-cache paths must be recorded.
Any repair after a failed held-out score returns to a new contract or diagnostic; it cannot overwrite the held-out result.

shams and ablations include confidence memory recurrent cost
metrics include utility regret latency calibration
